behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is mainly module handoff (history selection/order/serialization into the LLM request), not model judgment. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat service with a 12+ message history using a stubbed LLM client, then assert the outbound LLM payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order and excludes older ones.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM output-quality validation (relevance/on-topicness). Deterministic stubs would hide the exact failure, since they cannot test real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model calls on a representative prompt set and score whether replies answer the asked question (not generic/off-topic), with pass thresholds and repeated runs to account for variance.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment of tone (too formal vs acceptable). While orchestration exists, this behavior is centered on a single AI decision driving a branch.
behaviors.test_description: Feed casual-tier scenarios with intentionally formal drafts through the real tone-check path and verify the system flags them and invokes regeneration for those cases.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is persistence wiring/state propagation across app modules and DB layer; a local real DB readback provides the needed reality signal. No real external HTTP/subprocess or model-quality dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a request path (including a regeneration case) against the app with a real local test DB, then query persisted records to verify all turns are stored with correct timestamp ordering and fields.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: This verifies multi-step agentic sequencing and handoff correctness across initial generation -> tone decision -> regeneration -> final customer-visible output. A lower-level isolated test can miss leakage in real pipeline sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full live pipeline where tone-check triggers regeneration, then assert the customer-facing output/API only contains the regenerated response while audit logs retain the rejected draft internally.
